1.question: Awareness — do users know discovery features exist?

themes.summary: Users feel Spotify prioritizes money over experience

quotes.text: Don’t know even know where Spotify even began to purge downhill, but it did. Everything is incredibly money grabbing now. Why do I have to get premium to skip songs, queue songs, choose what I want to listen to, AND look at the lyrics. You can AT LEAST let us look at the lyrics, since it was always free up until around two years ago, which, by the way, seems greedy and money-hungry. Getting premium is practically a must at this point, considering you’ve taken away all the freedoms we once had, a

This app has continued to disappoint in the last few months. As they’ve added more and more unwanted content… audio books etc. it’s become slower and slower. Its core purpose is to download and listen to music. It’s glitchy now on ‘downloaded’ playlists on different devices and often can’t access downloaded content offline to listen to. It’s also performing poorly on iPad… have recently replaced to new iPad and still the same problems. Family and friends are also all having problems, not all bas

Tbh I wanted to rate it 0 but there’s no option cuz istg this is the most stupidest ever app.. they just wanna make money by shoeing and adds. I literally just changed a song and everytime I do that a add comes up and also the app is really not build for iPads it takes hours to reload and the app works really slow… I’ve been using Spotify since a long time and at the beginning the used to be very nice but now the quality goes way low. Using this app is now a hassle for me.it takes sooo longgggg

I love that we can listen to any music that is on there, game soundtracks, audiobooks, podcasts, etc. I also love that we can make several playlists and the app will suggest songs that match the vibe. 

However, one thing that sometimes gets annoying, is that for people who either can't afford Premium or have something holding them back from getting it, they have a small amount of chances to choose a song they want to listen to right there and then. 

I understand that you need a way to get peop

It takes 5 minutes to get a song to load with good wifi and even longer to load liked songs. I didn’t have this issue before it updated. What’s the point in paying for premium listening when I’m only able to load a handful of songs on a long commute? It’s so awkward at parties being like “yeah let’s get this party started!” and proceeding to listen to silence for 5 minutes

themes.summary: Users struggle with search and discovery

quotes.text:
Search has always been bad - but as the offering increases, you’re often stuck with the first tranche of results with no concept of page two. Adding information to the search usually just adds bad results rather reordering good ones to the top.

There’s no real way see information from a long title. Anything too far left is forever invisible. Tables visible on the desktop app aren’t to be found on the mobile app! Why? Then again in the absence of columns sorting and filtering- tables on the desk

I understand you kind of need it to make money but its way too selfish on Spotify's part because the user is unable to simply pick songs they want to listen too and they have to deal with the same ads repeating constantly and interrupting far too often. People will stop using apps when the apps get too greedy,

Title: This app has become terrible! Review: Spotify used to be great, but now it is completely ruined. There are way too many annoying ads. Whenever I search for a specific song, it forces me to listen to a whole playlist instead of that song. On top of that, you can only skip 6 songs in 2 hours! It is incredibly frustrating just trying to listen to one single song. This app seriously needs an urgent update and major improvements, otherwise, it is completely useless. Fix this or close it down!

I have had this app for a couple days now and it has been without fail one of the best apps i have and i just LOVE IT!!! I love travelling to it and it just is so relaxing. 5 stars. You should definitely get it!!
The app is as you always say constantly making changes and improvements. Despite that, the functionality isn't the best yet. I am very honest in my ratings and opinions. You can always do better.

2.question: Trust — do users believe recommendations are for them?

quotes.text: I’ve been listening to Southern mysteries for quite some time and decided to go ahead and write a review. It’s been most intriguing. I’ve always wanted to be like a forensic investigator. This series, which is researched by just one person is just mind-boggling. She’s done an amazing job with presenting mysteries and debit in such a way that’s just captivate the audience, well done.

I love everything about it, but there was just one tiny itty-bitty thing. I don’t really like about it. It’s that whenever you watch a video or a podcast or whatever you want it always freezes and you have to pause it to make it work or you just have to pick a different thing and I don’t really like that but that’s fine. I still love the music.

I really like the recommendation algorithm. It pushes me towards great music I’d never find otherwise. The amount of new / additional services prominent on the landing page however is annoying and in danger of becoming off putting noise.

themes.summary: Users love Spotify for personal music experiences

quotes.text:
Sure, without the dash:

Spotify feels less like a music app and more like a memory box. Every playlist holds a version of me: road trips, evening walks, happy days, heartbreaks, and moments when I simply sat staring at the sky. The best thing about Spotify isn’t the millions of songs; it’s how easily it finds the soundtrack for whatever I’m feeling. Whether I want comfort, excitement, nostalgia, or peace, it’s always there waiting with the right song. ★★★★★

Been with Spotify since 2007 and have been in love ever since! Only thing that could make it better is if they were able to get all of Garth Brooks music and Garth Brooks is actually singing not a tribute band. Help me out Spotify! Been a loyal customer for 9 years……give me the real Garth Brooks lol

hence the 4 stars instead of 5.  I have used Spotify for several years and it is great. However, it is still missing one feature that users have asked for time and time again on the Spotify community and that is to allow users to save albums into genre folders. We of course cannot forget about the podcasts included on Spotify! There are so many fun podcasts and a perfect one for any interest. I cannot say anything bad about this app unless you count how addictive I am to it!! P

4.question: Relevance — too safe, too random, or stuck in a loop?

quotes.text: I’ve been listening to Southern mysteries for quite some time and decided to go ahead and write a review. It’s been most intriguing. I’ve always wanted to be like a forensic investigator. This series, which is researched by just one person is just mind-boggling. She’s done an amazing job with presenting mysteries and debit in such a way that’s just captivate the audience, well done.

I love everything about it, but there was just one tiny itty-bitty thing. I don’t really like about it. It’s that whenever you watch a video or a podcast or whatever you want it always freezes and you have to pause it to make it work or you just have to pick a different thing and I don’t really like that but that’s fine. I still love the music.

I really like the recommendation algorithm. It pushes me towards great music I’d never find otherwise. The amount of new / additional services prominent on the landing page however is annoying and in danger of becoming off putting noise.

Spotify I have a question for you. Why do you think I make my own playlist? 

To play my OWN songs. So why are you giving me over 10 recommendations in a row when my playlist isn’t even finished. If I wanted recommendations I would play a bloody playlist by Spotify. I am getting really frustrated because I don’t want t

As a Spotify premium user, I don't have much to complain about. But, the one thing that made me rate this 2-stars (hopefully, y'all need to take such feedback very seriously) is the "shuffle" algorithm. It is VERY DISAPPOINTING! I left Spotify for almost 2 years and recently decided to go back to it and this made me remember why I left the app. Having 206 songs in a playlist and Spotify "shuffle-play" decides, that playing the same 23 songs is a good thing ??😤 Y'ALL NEED TO DO BETTER!

5.question: Context — do recommendations fit the moment?

quotes.text: I’ve been listening to Southern mysteries for quite some time and decided to go ahead and write a review. It’s been most intriguing. I’ve always wanted to be like a forensic investigator. This series, which is researched by just one person is just mind-boggling. She’s done an amazing job with presenting mysteries and debit in such a way that’s just captivate the audience, well done.

I love everything about it, but there was just one tiny itty-bitty thing. I don’t really like about it. It’s that whenever you watch a video or a podcast or whatever you want it always freezes and you have to pause it to make it work or you just have to pick a different thing and I don’t really like that but that’s fine. I still love the music.

I really like the recommendation algorithm. It pushes me towards great music I’d never find otherwise. The amount of new / additional services prominent on the landing page however is annoying and in danger of becoming off putting noise.

themes.summary: Users love Spotify for personal music experiences

Sure, without the dash:

Spotify feels less like a music app and more like a memory box. The best thing about Spotify isn’t the millions of songs; it’s how easily it finds the soundtrack for whatever I’m feeling. themes.summary: Users feel Spotify prioritizes money over experience

quotes.text: Don’t know even know where Spotify even began to purge downhill, but it did. Everything is incredibly money grabbing now. Why do I have to get premium to skip songs, queue songs, choose what I want to listen to, AND look at the lyrics. You can AT LEAST let us look at the lyrics, since it was always free up until around two years ago, which, by the way, seems greedy and money-hungry. Getting premium is practically a must at this point, considering you’ve taken away all the freedoms we once had, a

This app has continued to disappoint in the last few months. As they’ve added more and more unwanted content… audio books etc. it’s become slower and slower. Its core purpose is to download and listen to music. It’s glitchy now on ‘downloaded’ playlists on different devices and often can’t access downloaded content offline to listen to. It’s also performing poorly on iPad… have recently replaced to new iPad and still the same problems. Family and friends are also all having problems, not all bas

Tbh I wanted to rate it 0 but there’s no option cuz istg this is the most stupidest ever app.. they just wanna make money by shoeing and adds. I literally just changed a song and everytime I do that a add comes up and also the app is really not build for iPads it takes hours to reload and the app works really slow… I’ve been using Spotify since a long time and at the beginning the used to be very nice but now the quality goes way low. Using this app is now a hassle for me.it takes sooo longgggg

I love that we can listen to any music that is on there, game soundtracks, audiobooks, podcasts, etc. I also love that we can make several playlists and the app will suggest songs that match the vibe. 

However, one thing that sometimes gets annoying, is that for people who either can't afford Premium or have something holding them back from getting it, they have a small amount of chances to choose a song they want to listen to right there and then. 

I understand that you need a way to get peop

It takes 5 minutes to get a song to load with good wifi and even longer to load liked songs. I didn’t have this issue before it updated. What’s the point in paying for premium listening when I’m only able to load a handful of songs on a long commute? It’s so awkward at parties being like “yeah let’s get this party started!” and proceeding to listen to silence for 5 minutes

it only sucks if you dont have premium benefits but from my experience this is peak, although there was this song I forgot about that I'll put in later and edit that you guys deleted and im just asking to bring it back because it wasnt rude or anything, it was quite popular and it was my gf favourite song

Originally i would use this app very often but after they decided to become greedy and power hungry ive stopped using it. Music apps like this should not have things like this premium bs. I understand you kind of need it to make money but its way too selfish on Spotify's part because the user is unable to simply pick songs they want to listen too and they have to deal with the same ads repeating constantly and interrupting far too often. People will stop using apps when the apps get too greedy,

Title: This app has become terrible! Review: Spotify used to be great, but now it is completely ruined. There are way too many annoying ads. Whenever I search for a specific song, it forces me to listen to a whole playlist instead of that song. On top of that, you can only skip 6 songs in 2 hours! It is incredibly frustrating just trying to listen to one single song. This app seriously needs an urgent update and major improvements, otherwise, it is completely useless. Fix this or close it down!

Look I understand the hype for Spotify. I love it myself, but I don't use it often for reasons I wanna list simply. First one is why do I need premium just to loop a song, rewind a song all that? I feel like that should be for everyone considering all other Music apps allows that to everyone without paying. Second thing is why do y'all randomly shuffle music? Like when I choose a song it plays something completely different. It's stuck on shuffle, because again we have to pay to not feel too "li

Spotify I have a question for you. Why do you think I make my own playlist? 

To play my OWN songs. So why are you giving me over 10 recommendations in a row when my playlist isn’t even finished. If I wanted recommendations I would play a bloody playlist by Spotify. But I DON’T want your stupid recommendations when the music you recommend isn’t even to my liking. I use Spotify free so I have to use all my skips on your stupid recommendations. I am getting really frustrated because I don’t want t

As a Spotify premium user, I don't have much to complain about. But, the one thing that made me rate this 2-stars (hopefully, y'all need to take such feedback very seriously) is the "shuffle" algorithm. It is VERY DISAPPOINTING! I left Spotify for almost 2 years and recently decided to go back to it and this made me remember why I left the app. Having 206 songs in a playlist and Spotify "shuffle-play" decides, that playing the same 23 songs is a good thing ??😤 Y'ALL NEED TO DO BETTER!
